name: Cala Mesquida
description: A beach of pale sand and calm water just outside Maó, with a small cluster of fishermen's houses, restaurants and a defensive tower. It sits on the east coast —not the turquoise south—: Maó's local swim, less postcard and more everyday island life.

highlights: the pale-sand beach with calm waters 10 min from Maó
the defence tower above the cove
eating at one of its beachside fish restaurants
